text.source_text: अत्र केचिदुदाहरन्ति — ‘ प्राणस्य प्राणम् ’ ‘ प्राणबन्धनं हि सोम्य मनः ’ इति च । तदयुक्तम् ; शब्दभेदात्प्रकरणाच्च संशयानुपपत्तेः । यथा पितुः पितेति प्रयोगे , अन्यः पिता षष्ठीनिर्दिष्टात् प्रथमानिर्दिष्टः , पितुः पिता इति गम्यते । तद्वत् ‘ प्राणस्य प्राणम् ’ इति शब्दभेदात्प्रसिद्धात्प्राणात् अन्यः प्राणस्य प्राण इति निश्चीयते । न हि स एव तस्येति भेदनिर्देशार्हो भवति । यस्य च प्रकरणे यो निर्दिश्यते नामान्तरेणापि स एव तत्र प्रकरणी निर्दिष्ट इति गम्यते ; यथा ज्योतिष्टोमाधिकारे ‘ वसन्ते वसन्ते ज्योतिषा यजेत ’ इत्यत्र ज्योतिःशब्दो ज्योतिष्टोमविषयो भवति , तथा परस्य ब्रह्मणः प्रकरणे ‘ प्राणबन्धनं हि सोम्य मनः ’ इति श्रुतः प्राणशब्दो वायुविकारमात्रं कथमवगमयेत् । अतः संशयाविषयत्वान्नैतदुदाहरणं युक्तम् । प्रस्तावदेवतायां तु प्राणे संशयपूर्वपक्षनिर्णया उपपादिताः ॥ २३ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: atra kecidudāharanti — ‘ prāṇasya prāṇam ’ ‘ prāṇabandhanaṃ hi somya manaḥ ’ iti ca | tadayuktam ; śabdabhedātprakaraṇācca saṃśayānupapatteḥ | yathā pituḥ piteti prayoge , anyaḥ pitā ṣaṣṭhīnirdiṣṭāt prathamānirdiṣṭaḥ , pituḥ pitā iti gamyate | tadvat ‘ prāṇasya prāṇam ’ iti śabdabhedātprasiddhātprāṇāt anyaḥ prāṇasya prāṇa iti niścīyate | na hi sa eva tasyeti bhedanirdeśārho bhavati | yasya ca prakaraṇe yo nirdiśyate nāmāntareṇāpi sa eva tatra prakaraṇī nirdiṣṭa iti gamyate ; yathā jyotiṣṭomādhikāre ‘ vasante vasante jyotiṣā yajeta ’ ityatra jyotiḥśabdo jyotiṣṭomaviṣayo bhavati , tathā parasya brahmaṇaḥ prakaraṇe ‘ prāṇabandhanaṃ hi somya manaḥ ’ iti śrutaḥ prāṇaśabdo vāyuvikāramātraṃ kathamavagamayet | ataḥ saṃśayāviṣayatvānnaitadudāharaṇaṃ yuktam | prastāvadevatāyāṃ tu prāṇe saṃśayapūrvapakṣanirṇayā upapāditāḥ || 23 ||

translation.literal_translation: Some cite examples such as 'prana of prana' and 'mind indeed bound to prana.' That is not suitable as an example here, because by word-difference and by context there is no real doubt there. As in 'father of father,' the nominative father is understood as different from the genitive-indicated father; so in 'pranasya pranam,' a prana different from the familiar prana is determined, since one thing is not fit to be specified as its own different support in that way. And what is indicated in a context, even when named differently, is understood as that contextual topic - as in jyotistoma contexts where jyotis refers to the ritual. Thus in a context about Supreme Brahman, how could 'mind bound to prana' denote mere air-modification? Therefore those are not proper doubt-generating examples. In the prastava-deity case, however, doubt-purvapaksha-decision is properly established. (23)
translation.technical_translation: Bhashya clarifies scope: prior 'prana of prana' citations are contextually determinate and not true ambiguity cases; present adhikarana uniquely requires linga-based adjudication.
translation.interpretive_note: Methodological cleanup distinguishes real from only apparent lexical ambiguity.

lexical_notes.gloss: discrimination of the phrase 'prana of prana'
lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Methodological cleanup: not every prana-phrase creates genuine ambiguity in the same way as the present passage.
Sutra rejects false equivalence where wording and contextual setting already differ.
The surrounding topic controls whether a term is genuinely open to multiple readings.
Illustrative grammar shows how a genitive-plus-nominative pattern fixes the intended relation.

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S01 pass with stable inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit truncated source lines and detailed jyotis/gayatri technical phrasing in records 1.1.084-1.1.087.
